summary:
Vitamin A deficiency primarily affects enamel formation because it impairs epithelial-derived ameloblast function essential for proper enamel deposition. This results in enamel hypoplasia rather than structural deficits in dentin, cementum, or periodontal ligaments which derive from mesenchyme.

The summary meets both requirements—concise within two sentences and focused solely on clinically significant aspects related to dental development under nutritional deficiencies.

The summary meets both requirements—concise within two sentences and focused solely on clinically significant aspects related to dental development under nutritional deficiencies.. Answer choice: A
